body_txt: The band is rocking, everyone's cheering. They get to the call-and-response part of the song and.... nobody responds. The song's not famous enough, or the audience isn't feeling it, or what have you, but suddenly the whole vibe is ruined — or possibly made funnier. The traditional sound effect to indicate this is Chirping Crickets . There may also be a Sweat Drop to show the performer is feeling nerves, or a tumbleweed rolling by, or just a long awkward Beat of silence. If the performer is lucky, it may just be the moment before a Slow Clap begins. Of course, they're probably not lucky . A subversion of Audience Participation . See also Outside Joke . This may also occur when a bad comedian who Cannot Tell a Joke also falls flat at crowd work. Compare The Heckler , a person in the audience who “participates” by disrupting the performance with mockery. Also compare Dude, Not Funny! , when the audience finds a joke offensive instead of humorous. Contrast Fake Interactivity , when the audience is asked to respond but it doesn't really make a difference if they don't. May overlap with Cringe Comedy and Anti-Humor . Also related to Tough Room , when a quip that would probably get a laugh in real life doesn't get one in-story.
